title: Find Unknown Factors
essential_question: How can you find an unknown factor in a multiplication equation?
strategies: Think: '? times the known factor equals the product.'
Use a related multiplication fact or skip count.
Use an array to model: rows × columns = total.

pretest.explanation: Think: how many groups of 3 make 12? 4 × 3 = 12, so n = 4.
pretest.question: n × 3 = 12. What is n?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.2 'Find Unknown Factors' pp.193-196

learn.title: What Is an Unknown Factor?
learn.content: In the equation n × 3 = 12, the unknown factor is n. You need to find what number times 3 equals 12. The letter (n, m, x, etc.) is a placeholder for the missing factor.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.2 'Find Unknown Factors' pp.193-196
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as an Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Strategy: Skip Count to Find It
learn.content: To find n in n × 5 = 30, skip count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 — that is 6 jumps. So n = 6. Or use a known fact: 6 × 5 = 30.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.2 'Find Unknown Factors' pp.193-196
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as an Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Strategy: Use an Array
learn.content: For 3 × □ = 24, draw 3 rows. Keep adding columns until you reach 24 dots total — you will get 8 columns. So □ = 8. Arrays make the unknown factor visible.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.2 'Find Unknown Factors' pp.193-196
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as an Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Division Is the Reverse
learn.content: Finding an unknown factor is the same as dividing. If n × 6 = 42, then n = 42 ÷ 6 = 7. Division 'undoes' multiplication. You can always use this shortcut to solve quickly.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.2 'Find Unknown Factors' pp.193-196
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as an Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Always Check Your Answer
learn.content: After finding the unknown factor, substitute it back: if n = 7, check n × 6 = 7 × 6 = 42 ✓. If it matches the product, your answer is correct. This works for any unknown factor equation.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.2 'Find Unknown Factors' pp.193-196
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as an Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 사실 가족(Fact Family) — 곱셈과 나눗셈은 한 가족
learn.content: 세 수 (6, 7, 42)는 4개의 식을 만듭니다 — '사실 가족'. 곱셈: 6 × 7 = 42, 7 × 6 = 42. 나눗셈: 42 ÷ 6 = 7, 42 ÷ 7 = 6. 미지수를 찾을 때 활용: 6 × ? = 42에서 가족 멤버 7이 답. 팁: 사실 가족 삼각형 — 위에 곱(42), 아래에 두 인자(6, 7). 어느 모서리가 가려지면 그게 미지수. 곱이 가려지면 ×, 인자가 가려지면 ÷.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.2 'Find Unknown Factors' pp.193-196
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as an Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 검증 — 답을 식에 다시 넣기
learn.content: 미지수 답을 찾았으면 반드시 원식에 다시 넣어 확인. 예: 7 × ? = 42에서 ? = 6이라 답했으면 — 7 × 6 = 42 ✓ 검증. 잘못된 답: ? = 5라 답하면 7 × 5 = 35 ≠ 42 ❌. 이 단계 없으면 '느낌 답'으로 자주 틀림 (M03 random_guess). 검증은 1초밖에 안 걸리지만 정답률을 크게 올림. 흔한 실수: 검증 없이 첫 답을 그대로 제출.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.2 'Find Unknown Factors' pp.193-196
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as an Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 미지수 인자 풀이 4단계 + 검증
learn.content: 단계 1 식 분석: a × ? = b 또는 b = a × ?. 단계 2 무엇이 가려졌는지 — 인자(둘 중 하나)? 단계 3 나눗셈 적용: ? = b ÷ a.   예: 7 × ? = 42 → ? = 42 ÷ 7 = 6. 단계 4 검증: 7 × 6 = 42 ✓. 🔍 양변 어디든 미지수 가능: 56 = 8 × ? ↔ 8 × ? = 56 (같은 의미). 교환법칙 활용: 9 × ? = 63 모르면 ? × 9 = 63으로 바꿔 ×9표 떠올리기.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.2 'Find Unknown Factors' pp.193-196
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as an Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Ask: '7 × ? = 21'. Which fact do you know?
Skip count by 7: 7, 14, 21 — how many jumps?

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Ask: 7 × ? = 21. Count by 7s: 7, 14, 21 — that is 3 jumps, so n = 3.

try.question: 21 = 7 × n. What is n?

practice_r3.explanation: n × 8 = 48 → n = 6 teams. 8 students per team is between 5 and 9. ✓
hints: Find a factor of 48 that is between 5 and 9 — that is the team size.
Try: 48 ÷ 8 = 6 teams, 8 per team. Is 8 between 5 and 9?

feedback.correct: Correct! 6 × 8 = 48 and 8 students per team is between 5 and 9.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Try 48 ÷ 8 = 6 teams with 8 per team. 8 is between 5 and 9 ✓.

practice_r3.question: A gym teacher has 48 students. She wants to make equal teams with more than 5 but fewer than 9 students per team. How many teams can she make?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! 6 × 8 = 48 and 8 students per team is between 5 and 9.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.2 'Find Unknown Factors' pp.193-196
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as an Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Set up the equation: n boxes × 8 markers each = 64 markers total.
Solve: 64 ÷ 8 = ? and then check your answer.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Set up: n × 8 = 64. Solve: 64 ÷ 8 = 8, so n = 8.

practice_r3.question: Sarah buys n boxes of markers. Each box has 8 markers. She has 64 markers in all. Which equation and solution is correct?

practice_r3.explanation: 7 × 6 = 42 ✓. Her method and answer are both correct.
hints: Substituting n = 7 back: 7 × 6 = 42. Does it match?

feedback.correct: Correct! 7 × 6 = 42 ✓. Tina's method of checking by substituting is right.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Check: 7 × 6 = 42 ✓. Tina's answer and method are both correct.

practice_r3.question: Tina says 'If n × 6 = 42, then n = 7. I can check by computing 7 × 6.' Is she correct?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! 7 × 6 = 42 ✓. Tina's method of checking by substituting is right.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. n × 2 = 56 → 56 ÷ 2 = 28 cards per friend.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. n × 9 = 63. Solve: 63 ÷ 9 = 7, so n = 7 layers.
